steps.title: Convert years to model inputs
steps.content: Use years after $1993$ as the input. Then $1993$ becomes $0$, $2013$ becomes $20$, and $2017$ becomes $24$. The two known data points are $(0,64)$ and $(20,238)$, with population measured in thousands.

steps.title: Find the linear model
steps.content: Use the two known points to find the slope: $$m=\frac{238-64}{20-0}=\frac{174}{20}=8.7$$ Since the point with input $0$ has output $64$, the intercept is $64$. The model is $$y=8.7x+64.$$

steps.title: Evaluate at 2017
steps.content: For $2017$, use $x=24$: $$y=8.7(24)+64=272.8.$$ Rounded to the nearest whole number, the estimated population is 273 thousand.
